Ayahs

ٱلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ فَاطِرِ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ جَاعِلِ ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةِ رُسُلًا أُو۟لِىٓ أَجْنِحَةٍ مَّثْنَىٰ وَثُلَـٰثَ وَرُبَـٰعَ ۚ يَزِيدُ فِى ٱلْخَلْقِ مَا يَشَآءُ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (١)
All praise belongs to Allah, originator of the heavens and the earth, maker of the angels [His] messengers, possessing wings, two, three or four [of them]. He adds to the creation whatever He wishes. Indeed Allah has power over all things. (1)
مَّا يَفْتَحِ ٱللَّهُ لِلنَّاسِ مِن رَّحْمَةٍ فَلَا مُمْسِكَ لَهَا ۖ وَمَا يُمْسِكْ فَلَا مُرْسِلَ لَهُۥ مِنۢ بَعْدِهِۦ ۚ وَهُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ (٢)
Whatever mercy Allah unfolds for the people, no one can withhold it; and whatever He withholds no one can release it except Him, and He is the All-mighty, the All-wise. (2)
يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ ٱذْكُرُوا۟ نِعْمَتَ ٱللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ ۚ هَلْ مِنْ خَـٰلِقٍ غَيْرُ ٱللَّهِ يَرْزُقُكُم مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ۖ فَأَنَّىٰ تُؤْفَكُونَ (٣)
O mankind! Remember Allah’s blessing upon you! Is there any creator other than Allah who provides for you from the sky and the earth? There is no god except Him. So where do you stray? (3)

Content of Surah Fatir

This Surah, which is sometimes called Surah "Fatir" and sometimes Surah "Mala'ikah" (because of its beginning which starts with the titles "Fatir" and "Mala'ikah"), is among the Meccan Surahs, although some have excepted two of its verses and considered them Medinan (verses 29 and 32); however, there is no clear evidence for this exception.

Since this Surah is Meccan, the general content of Meccan Surahs—namely, discourse on the "Origin" (Mabda') and "Resurrection" (Ma'ad), the "struggle against polytheism," the call of the messengers' mission, the mention of the Lord's blessings, and the fate of criminals on the Day of Recompense—is fully reflected in it.

The verses of this Surah can be summarized into five sections:

  1. A significant portion of the verses in this Surah speaks about the signs of Allah's greatness in the universe and the proofs of Monotheism (Tawhid).

  2. Another part discusses the Lordship (Rububiyyah) of the Lord and His governance over the entire world and specifically over man, His role as Creator and Provider, the creation of man from dust, and his stages of evolution.

  3. Another section concerns the Resurrection and the results of deeds in the Hereafter, the vast Divine mercy in this world, and His infallible Sunnah (tradition) regarding the arrogant.

  4. A portion of its verses also refers to the issue of the leadership of the Prophets and their continuous and persistent struggle against stubborn and headstrong enemies, and the comforting of the Prophet of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله in this regard.

  5. Finally, a part of it expresses Divine exhortations and advice in various fields, which complement the previous discussions.

Some commentators have summarized this entire Surah into one cycle, which is the issue of Allah's Sovereignty (Qahiriyyah) in all fields. [^1] Although this statement seems appropriate considering a significant portion of the Surah's verses, the existence of other various sections in this Surah cannot be denied.

The Virtue of this Surah

In a Hadith from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, it is stated:

من قراء سورة الملائكه دعته يوم القيامة ثلاثه ابواب من الجنة ان ادخل من اى الأبواب شئت!:

"Whoever recites Surah al-Mala'ikah, on the Day of Resurrection, three doors from the doors of Paradise will call him to themselves: 'Enter from whichever door you wish!'" [^2] Considering that we know the doors of Paradise are the very beliefs and righteous deeds that cause one to reach Paradise—just as in some narrations a door is mentioned as the "Gate of the Mujahideen" or the like—it is possible that this narration refers to the three gates of belief in Monotheism, the Resurrection, and the Mission of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله.

In another Hadith from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام, we read: "There are two Surahs in the Glorious Quran (placed consecutively), Surah Saba and Surah Fatir, which begin with 'Al-Hamdu lillah' (Praise be to Allah). Whoever recites them at night, Allah will preserve him under His protection,

[^1]: Tafsir "Fi Zilal," beginning of Surah Fatir.

[^2]: "Majma' al-Bayan," beginning of Surah Fatir.

and whoever recites them during the day, no distress shall reach him, and Allah will grant him so much of the good of this world and the Hereafter that it has not crossed anyone's heart, nor has anyone's wish reached it." [^1] As we have said before, the Quran is a program for action, and its recitation is a beginning for reflection and faith, which in turn is a means for acting upon its content; all these great rewards are realized from this point and with these conditions (reflect carefully).

[^1]: Thawab al-A'mal, as quoted in Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 4, p. 345.

بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ‌

الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ فاطِرِ السَّماواتِ وَ الْأَرْضِ جاعِلِ الْمَلائِكَةِ رُسُلاً أُولِي أَجْنِحَةٍ مَثْنى‌ وَ ثُلاثَ وَ رُباعَ يَزِيدُ فِي الْخَلْقِ ما يَشاءُ إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَلى‌ كُلِّ شَيْ‌ءٍ قَدِيرٌ (1) ما يَفْتَحِ اللَّهُ لِلنَّاسِ مِنْ رَحْمَةٍ فَلا مُمْسِكَ لَها وَ ما يُمْسِكْ فَلا مُرْسِلَ لَهُ مِنْ بَعْدِهِ وَ هُوَ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ (2) يا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ اذْكُرُوا نِعْمَتَ اللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ هَلْ مِنْ خالِقٍ غَيْرُ اللَّهِ يَرْزُقُكُمْ مِنَ السَّماءِ وَ الْأَرْضِ لا إِلهَ إِلاَّ هُوَ فَأَنَّى تُؤْفَكُونَ (3)

Commentary

He is the Opener of Closed Doors!

The beginning of this Surah—like Surahs "Hamd," "Saba," and "Kahf"—starts with the praise of the Lord. His praise and glorification are for the creation of the vast universe of existence; He says: "Praise belongs to Allah, the Creator of the heavens and the earth," and all blessings and bounties of existence originate from His generous Being (الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ فاطِرِ السَّماواتِ وَ الْأَرْضِ‌).

"Fatir" is from the root "futur," which originally means "splitting." Since the creation of beings is like the splitting of the darkness of non-existence and the emergence of the light of existence, this expression is used regarding creation and origination. Especially considering modern sciences which state that the entire universe was initially a single mass that gradually split and parts of it were separated, the application of the word "Fatir" to the Pure Essence of Allah takes on a newer and clearer meaning. [^1] Yes, we praise and thank Him for His Creatorship, for whatever exists is from Him, and no one besides Him possesses anything of their own.

And since the governance of this world by the Lord—by virtue of the fact that the world is a world of causes—has been entrusted to the angels, He immediately speaks of their creation and the great powers that the Lord has placed at their disposal.

"Allah Who made the angels messengers, possessing wings, two, three,

[^1]: Regarding the meaning of "Fatir" and "Futur," we have also spoken in Vol. 10, p. 287 (under verse 10 of Surah Ibrahim) and also in Vol. 5, p. 171 (under verse 14 of al-An'am).

and four" (جاعِلِ الْمَلائِكَةِ رُسُلًا أُولِي أَجْنِحَةٍ مَثْنى‌ وَ ثُلاثَ وَ رُباعَ‌).

Then He adds: "Allah adds to creation whatever He wills, for He is Powerful and Capable over all things" (يَزِيدُ فِي الْخَلْقِ ما يَشاءُ إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَلى‌ كُلِّ شَيْ‌ءٍ قَدِيرٌ).

Here three questions arise: First, what is the mission (risalah) of the angels mentioned in the above verse? Is it a legislative mission (risalah tashri'iyyah)? That is, bringing messages from Allah to the Prophets. Or is it a cosmic mission (risalah takwiniyyah)? That is, undertaking various assignments in the world of creation, as will be mentioned in the discussion of points. Or is it both aspects?

Considering that the previous sentence spoke of the creation of the heavens and the earth, and the sentence under discussion speaks of the multiple wings of the angels which are signs of their power, and also considering that He has attributed the title of "messengers" to all angels (note that "al-Mala'ikah" is a plural accompanied by the definite article "al," which gives a general meaning), it seems that "risalah" (mission) here is used in a broad and extensive sense that includes both the "legislative mission" and the "cosmic mission."

The application of "risalah" to the "legislative mission" and bringing the message of revelation to the Prophets is frequent in the Quran, but its application to the "cosmic mission" is also not rare.

In verse 21 of Surah Yunus, we read: ان ارسلنا يكتبون ما تمكرون: "Our messengers (Our angels) record what you plot."

And in verse 61 of al-An'am, we read: حَتَّى إِذا جاءَ أَحَدَكُمُ الْمَوْتُ تَوَفَّتْهُ رُسُلُنا: "Until when death comes to one of you, Our messengers take his soul."

In verse 31 of Surah al-Ankabut, regarding the angels who were commissioned to destroy the land of the people of Lot, it says: و لما جاءت‌رُسُلُنا إِبْراهِيمَ بِالْبُشْرى‌ قالُوا إِنَّا مُهْلِكُوا أَهْلِ هذِهِ الْقَرْيَةِ إِنَّ أَهْلَها كانُوا ظالِمِينَ‌: "When Our messengers came to Abraham with the glad tidings, they said: 'We are going to destroy the people of this town, for its people are wrongdoers.'"

In other verses of the Quran, we also see various missions entrusted to the angels which are considered their "risalahs"; therefore, "risalah" has a broad concept.

Secondly, what is meant by the wings of the angels, specifically wings of two, three, and four?

It is not unlikely that "wings" here refers to the power of maneuverability and the ability for activity, where some are superior to others and possess greater capability.

Hence, He has assigned a hierarchy of wings for them, where some have four wings (mathna—two by two), some have six wings, and some have eight wings.

"Ajnihah" is the plural of "janah," meaning the wing of birds, which is like the hand for a human. Since the wing is the means of transport for birds and their movement and activity, sometimes this word in Persian or Arabic is used as a metaphor for the means of movement and the exercise of power and ability. For example, it is said "so-and-so's wings were burnt," meaning the power of movement and ability was taken from him; or "so-and-so took him under his wing"; or "man must fly with the two wings of knowledge and action," and similar expressions which all indicate the metaphorical meaning of this word.

In other cases, expressions like "Arsh" (Throne), "Kursi" (Chair), "Lawh" (Tablet), and "Qalam" (Pen) are seen, where usually their spiritual concepts are intended, not their material physical bodies.

Of course, without evidence, Quranic terms cannot be interpreted as other than their literal meanings, but where clear evidence is involved, no problem arises.

In some narrations, it is stated: "Gabriel (the messenger of Allah's revelation) has six hundred wings! And when he met the Prophet of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله in this state, he filled the space between the earth and the sky!" [^1] Or that "Allah has an angel, the distance between whose earlobe and his eye is a five-hundred-year journey for a (swift) bird!" [^2] Or in Nahj al-Balagha, when speaking of the greatness of the Lord's angels, it says:

و منهم الثابتة فى الارضين السفلى اقدامهم، و المارقة من السماء العليا اعناقهم، و الخارجة من الاقطار اركانهم، و المناسبة لقوائم العرش اكتافهم:

"Some of the angels are so great that their feet are fixed in the lower layers of the earth and their necks are higher than the highest heaven; the pillars of their existence have gone beyond the regions of the world, and their shoulders are suited to carry the Throne of the Lord!" [^3] It is clear that such expressions cannot be interpreted as physical material aspects, but rather they express their spiritual greatness and the dimensions of their power.

Fundamentally, we know that wings are only used for movement in the earth's atmosphere, because the earth is surrounded by compressed air, and birds position themselves on air waves by means of their wings and can go up and down. But once we exit the earth's atmosphere where there is no air, wings have the slightest effect on movement, and in this regard, they are just like other limbs.

Furthermore, an angel whose feet are in the depths of the earth and whose head is higher than the highest heaven has no need for physical flight!

[^1]: and [^2]: Tafsir of Ali ibn Ibrahim as quoted in Nur al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 4, p. 349.

[^3]: "Nahj al-Balagha," Sermon 1.

The discussion of whether an "angel" is a subtle body or among the immaterial beings (mujarradat) is another discussion that will be referred to in the points, God willing. For now, the intention is to know that their wings are a means of activity, movement, and power, for which the above evidences are sufficiently expressive. Just as we said in the discussion of "Arsh" and "Kursi," although these two words mean "high-legged" and "short-legged" thrones, they certainly refer to the power of the Lord in various dimensions of the world.

In a Hadith from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام, it is stated:

الملائكه لا ياكلون و لا يشربون و لا ينكحون، و انما يعيشون بنسيم العرش:

"The angels neither eat nor drink nor marry; they only live by the breeze of the Throne!" [^1] and [^2] The third question is whether the sentence يَزِيدُ فِي الْخَلْقِ ما يَشاءُ: "He adds to His creation whatever He wills" refers to the increase in the wings of the angels, as some commentators have said? Or does it have a broad meaning that includes that as well as other increases that take place in the creation of beings?

The absolute nature of the sentence on one hand, and some Islamic narrations that have been reported in the interpretation of the above verses on the other, show that the second meaning is more appropriate.

Among them, in a Hadith from the Noble Prophet of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله, it is stated that in interpreting this sentence he said:

هو الوجه الحسن، و الصوت الحسن، و الشعر الحسن:

"It refers to a beautiful face, a beautiful voice, and beautiful hair!" [^3]

[^1]: Tafsir "Ali ibn Ibrahim" as quoted in Nur al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 4, p. 349.

[^2]: And we have discussed the meaning of "Arsh" in detail in Vol. 6, p. 204 (under verse 54 of Surah al-A'raf).

[^3]: "Majma' al-Bayan" under the verses in question; "Qurtubi" also cited this same Hadith in his Tafsir under the verse in question.

In another Hadith from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, we read:

حسنوا القرآن باصواتكم فان الصوت الحسن يزيد القرآن حسنا، و قرأيَزِيدُ فِي الْخَلْقِ ما يَشاءُ:

"Beautify the Quran with your voices, for a good voice adds to the beauty of the Quran," then he recited this verse: يَزِيدُ فِي الْخَلْقِ ما يَشاءُ.

After stating the Creatorship of the Lord and the mission of the angels who are the intermediaries of grace, He mentions His mercy, which is the foundation of the entire universe of existence; He says: "Whatever Allah opens for people of mercy, none can withhold it" (ما يَفْتَحِ اللَّهُ لِلنَّاسِ مِنْ رَحْمَةٍ فَلا مُمْسِكَ لَها).

"And whatever He withholds, none can release it after Him" (وَ ما يُمْسِكْ فَلا مُرْسِلَ لَهُ مِنْ بَعْدِهِ‌).

"For He is the All-Mighty, the Invincible, and at the same time, the All-Wise and Aware" (وَ هُوَ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ‌).

In short, all the treasures of mercy are with Him, and He makes whoever He deems worthy subject to it, and wherever His wisdom requires, He opens its doors. If all the inhabitants of the worlds joined hands to close a door He has opened, or to open a door He has closed, they would never be able to. This is, in fact, an important branch of Monotheism (Tawhid) which is the source of other branches (reflect carefully).

A similar meaning has appeared in other verses of the Quran, where it says:

وَ إِنْ يَمْسَسْكَ اللَّهُ بِضُرٍّ فَلا كاشِفَ لَهُ إِلَّا هُوَ وَ إِنْ يُرِدْكَ بِخَيْرٍ فَلا رَادَّ لِفَضْلِهِ يُصِيبُ بِهِ مَنْ يَشاءُ مِنْ عِبادِهِ وَ هُوَ الْغَفُورُ الرَّحِيمُ‌:

"If Allah touches you with harm (for trial or punishment for a mistake), none can remove it but He; and if He intends any good for you, none can

repel His favor. He causes it to reach whomsoever He wills of His servants, and He is the All-Forgiving, the All-Merciful" (Yunus-107).

Here, several "matters" must be noted

  1. The expression "yaftah" which is from the root "fath" meaning "to open" refers to the existence of the treasures of Divine mercy, which has also been mentioned in other verses of the Quran. Interestingly, these treasures are such that as soon as they are opened, they flow upon the creatures and there is no need for anything else, and no one can prevent it. Placing the opening of mercy before its withholding is because Allah's mercy always precedes His wrath.

  2. The term "mercy" (rahmah) has a very broad and extensive meaning that includes all the bounties of the world. Sometimes it has a spiritual aspect, and sometimes a material aspect. For this reason, sometimes when a person sees all external doors closed to him, he feels that Divine mercy is flowing into his heart and soul; thus he is happy and joyful, calm and confident, even if he is trapped in the narrowness of a prison. Conversely, sometimes a person sees all external doors open to him, but it is as if the door of Divine mercy has been closed in his soul; he feels himself in such narrowness and pressure that the world with all its vastness becomes a dark and terrifying prison for him. This is something that is felt and touched by many people.

  3. The expression of the two attributes "Al-Mighty" (Aziz) and "All-Wise" (Hakim) expresses His power over the "sending" and "withholding" of mercy, and at the same time, it points to the truth that this opening and closing is everywhere based on wisdom, for His power is blended with His wisdom.

In any case, paying attention to the content of this verse gives such peace to the believing person that he becomes resistant to all events; he fears no problem and is not deceived by any victory. [^1]

In the next verse, He refers to the issue of "Monotheism in Worship" based on "Monotheism in Creatorship and Provision," saying:

"O people, remember Allah's blessing upon you" (يا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ اذْكُرُوا نِعْمَتَ اللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ‌).

Think correctly: all these bounties and blessings, and all these vital facilities that have been placed at your disposal and in which you are immersed—who is their primary source and origin?! "Is there any creator other than Allah who provides for you from the heaven and the earth?" (هَلْ مِنْ خالِقٍ غَيْرُ اللَّهِ يَرْزُقُكُمْ مِنَ السَّماءِ وَ الْأَرْضِ‌).

Who sends the life-giving light of the sun, the life-restoring drops of rain, and the soul-nourishing waves of the breeze from the sky toward you? And who brings out the minerals and treasures of the earth, the food materials, and various types of plants, fruits, and other blessings from this earth for you?

Now that you know the source of all these blessings is Him, then know that "there is no deity but He, and worship and adoration are worthy only of His Pure Essence" (لا إِلهَ إِلَّا هُوَ).

"With all this, how are you turned away from the path of truth toward falsehood? And

[^1]: It is noteworthy that the pronoun in "fala mumsika laha" is feminine, and in "fala mursila lahu" it is masculine, because the referent of the first is the word "rahmah" (mercy), and the second is the word "ma" (whatever). Also, the pronoun in "min ba'dihi" apparently refers back to Allah, meaning after Allah no one is able to open—the possibility has also been given that the pronoun refers back to withholding, meaning after Allah's withholding, which does not differ much in meaning.

instead of Allah, you prostrate before idols?" (فانى تؤفكون).

"Tu'fakun" is from the root "ifk" (on the pattern of fikr). As we have said before, it is said of anything that is changed from its original state. Therefore, any speech that deviates from the truth is called "ifk," and the fact that we see it used to mean lie and slander is from this perspective; however, some believe this word expresses great lies and slanders.

Key Point: Angels in the Glorious Quran

In the Glorious Quran, angels are mentioned frequently. Many verses of the Quran speak about the attributes, characteristics, missions, and duties of the angels. The Quran even places faith in angels in the same rank as faith in Allah, the Prophets, and the Heavenly Books, and this is proof of the fundamental importance of this issue: آمَنَ الرَّسُولُ بِما أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْهِ مِنْ رَبِّهِ وَ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ كُلٌّ آمَنَ بِاللَّهِ وَ مَلائِكَتِهِ وَ كُتُبِهِ وَ رُسُلِهِ‌: "The Messenger has believed in what was revealed to him from his Lord, and the believers; all have believed in Allah and His angels and His books and His messengers" (Baqarah-285).

Undoubtedly, the existence of angels is among the matters of the Unseen (ghayb), for the proof of which, with these attributes and characteristics, there is no way except through traditional (naqli) evidences, and by virtue of faith in the Unseen, they must be accepted.

The Glorious Quran altogether counts their characteristics as follows: 1. Angels are rational and conscious beings and are honored servants of Allah (بَلْ عِبادٌ مُكْرَمُونَ‌) (Anbiya-26).

  1. They are submissive to Allah's command and never disobey Him (لا يَسْبِقُونَهُ بِالْقَوْلِ وَ هُمْ بِأَمْرِهِ يَعْمَلُونَ‌) (Anbiya-27).

  2. They have important and very diverse duties from Allah: A group are the carriers of the Throne (Haqqah-17). A group are the regulators of affairs (Nazi'at-5). A group are the angels who take souls (A'raf-37). A group are the observers of human deeds (Surah Infitar-10 to 13). A group are the protectors of man from dangers and accidents (An'am-61). A group are commissioned for the punishment and chastisement of rebellious nations (Hud-77). A group are Divine helpers to the believers in wars (Ahzab-9). And finally, a group are the communicators of revelation and the bringers of Heavenly Books to the Prophets (Nahl-2). If we were to count their duties and missions one by one, the discussion would become lengthy.

  3. They are constantly engaged in the glorification and sanctification of Allah, as we read in verse 5 of Surah ash-Shura: وَ الْمَلائِكَةُ يُسَبِّحُونَ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّهِمْ وَ يَسْتَغْفِرُونَ لِمَنْ فِي الْأَرْضِ‌: "The angels glorify with the praise of their Lord and ask forgiveness for those on earth."

  4. Despite this, man, according to his potential for perfection, is superior and higher than them, to the extent that all angels without exception fell in prostration because of the creation of Adam, and Adam became their teacher (verses 30-34 of Surah al-Baqarah).

  5. They sometimes take human form and appear to Prophets and even non-Prophets, as we read in Surah Maryam: the great Divine angel appeared to Maryam in the form of a well-proportioned man: فَأَرْسَلْنا إِلَيْها رُوحَنا فَتَمَثَّلَ لَها بَشَراً سَوِيًّا: (Maryam-17). In another place, they appeared in the form of humans to Abraham and to Lot (Hud-69 and 77). It is even understood from the end of these verses that the people of Lot also saw them in those same well-proportioned human forms (Hud-78). Is appearing in the face of a human an objective reality? Or is it in the form of "tamaththul" (representation) and influence on the power of perception? The literal meaning of the Quranic verses is the first meaning, although some great commentators have chosen the second meaning.

  6. It is understood from Islamic narrations that their number is so great that it is in no way comparable to humans, as we read in a narration from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام: When he was asked whether the number of angels is greater or humans? He said: "By Allah in Whose hand is my soul, the angels of Allah in the heavens are more than the number of particles of dust on the earth, and in the heaven there is no place for a foot except that there is an angel there glorifying and sanctifying Allah." [^1]

  7. They neither eat food nor drink water, nor do they have marriage, as we read in a Hadith from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام: ان الملائكة لا ياكلون و لا يشربون و لا ينكحون و انما يعيشون بنسيم العرش: "The angels do not eat food, nor do they drink water, nor do they marry, but rather they live by the breeze of the Divine Throne!" [^2]

  8. They have neither sleep nor lethargy nor negligence, as Ali عليه السلام says in a Hadith:

[^1]: "Bihar al-Anwar," Vol. 59, p. 176 (Hadith 7). Many other narrations have also been reported in this regard.

[^2]: "Bihar al-Anwar," Vol. 59, p. 174 (Hadith 4).

ليس فيهم فترة، و لا عندهم غفلة، و لا فيهم معصية ... لا يغشاهم نوم العيون و لا سهوا العقول، و لا فترة الأبدان، لم يسكنوا الاصلاب و لم تضمهم الارحام: "In them there is no lethargy, nor is there negligence with them, nor is there disobedience in them... sleep of the eyes does not overcome them, nor does the error of minds, nor the lethargy of bodies; they did not reside in loins nor were they contained by wombs." [^1]

  1. They have different stations and varying ranks; some are always in bowing (ruku') and some are always in prostration (sujud). ما مِنَّا إِلَّا لَهُ مَقامٌ مَعْلُومٌ وَ إِنَّا لَنَحْنُ الصَّافُّونَ وَ إِنَّا لَنَحْنُ الْمُسَبِّحُونَ‌: "There is not one of us but has a known station; and indeed, we are those who stand in ranks, and indeed, we are those who glorify" (Saffat 164-166). Imam al-Sadiq says: و ان للَّه ملائكة ركعا الى يوم القيامه و ان للَّه ملائكة سجد الى يوم القيامه: "Indeed, Allah has angels who are in bowing until the Day of Resurrection, and indeed, Allah has angels who are in prostration until the Day of Resurrection!" [^2] For more information on the attributes of angels and their categories, refer to the book "As-Sama' wa al-Alam" in Bihar al-Anwar, "Abwab al-Mala'ikah" (Vol. 59, pp. 144 to 326; also Nahj al-Balagha, Sermons 1, 91—Sermon of al-Ashbah—109, and 171).

With these attributes mentioned for the angels, are they immaterial (mujarrad) beings or material?! Undoubtedly, with these attributes, they cannot be of this dense elemental matter, but there is no obstacle to them being created from subtle bodies, bodies beyond this ordinary matter

[^1]: "Bihar al-Anwar," Vol. 59, p. 175.

[^2]: "Bihar al-Anwar," Vol. 59, p. 174.

with which we are familiar. Proving "absolute immateriality" for angels, even from time, place, and parts, is not an easy task, and researching this issue does not yield much benefit either. What is important is that we know the angels by the attributes described by the Quran and the established Islamic narrations and consider them a great category of Allah's high and prominent beings, without attributing any station to them other than the station of servitude and worship, or considering them partners with Allah in creation or worship, which is pure polytheism and disbelief.

In the discussion about angels, we suffice with this amount and leave the details to books specifically written in this field. In many expressions of the Torah, angels are referred to as "gods," which is a polytheistic expression and among the signs of the distortion of the current Torah; however, the Glorious Quran is pure and free from such expressions, for it has not assigned them any station other than servitude, worship, and the execution of Divine commands. Even as we said, it appears from various verses of the Quran that the station of the Perfect Man is higher and loftier than the angels.

Ayahs

وَإِن يُكَذِّبُوكَ فَقَدْ كُذِّبَتْ رُسُلٌ مِّن قَبْلِكَ ۚ وَإِلَى ٱللَّهِ تُرْجَعُ ٱلْأُمُورُ (٤)
If they impugn you, certainly [other] apostles were impugned before you, and all matters are returned to Allah. (4)
يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ إِنَّ وَعْدَ ٱللَّهِ حَقٌّ ۖ فَلَا تَغُرَّنَّكُمُ ٱلْحَيَوٰةُ ٱلدُّنْيَا ۖ وَلَا يَغُرَّنَّكُم بِٱللَّهِ ٱلْغَرُورُ (٥)
O mankind! Allah’s promise is indeed true. So do not let the life of the world deceive you, nor let the Deceiver deceive you concerning Allah. (5)
إِنَّ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنَ لَكُمْ عَدُوٌّ فَٱتَّخِذُوهُ عَدُوًّا ۚ إِنَّمَا يَدْعُوا۟ حِزْبَهُۥ لِيَكُونُوا۟ مِنْ أَصْحَـٰبِ ٱلسَّعِيرِ (٦)
Satan is indeed your enemy, so treat him as an enemy. He only invites his confederates so that they may be among the inmates of the Blaze. (6)
ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ شَدِيدٌ ۖ وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ لَهُم مَّغْفِرَةٌ وَأَجْرٌ كَبِيرٌ (٧)
There is a severe punishment for the faithless; but for those who have faith and do righteous deeds, there will be forgiveness and a great reward. (7)

Commentary

Let Not the World and Satan Deceive You!

In the second part of the verses of this Surah, following the discussion regarding the Oneness of the Creator and the Provider, the address is directed first to the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله) and then to the general public, outlining their practical programs following the previous doctrinal programs:

First, it gives the Prophet a lesson in steadfastness on his path, which is the most important lesson for him, stating: "And if they deny you, [O Muhammad] - already were messengers denied before you" (وَ إِنْ يُكَذِّبُوكَ فَقَدْ كُذِّبَتْ رُسُلٌ مِنْ قَبْلِكَ‌).

They too resisted on this path and did not rest until they had fulfilled their mission. You too must stand firm and fulfill your mission; the rest is with Allah.

"The important thing is that all matters return to Allah, and He is the Observer of everything and the Reckoner of all deeds" (وَ إِلَى اللَّهِ تُرْجَعُ الْأُمُورُ).

He will never overlook your efforts on this path, just as He will not leave the denials of these stubborn opponents unpunished. If there were no Day of Resurrection, there would be cause for concern, but given that Great Court and the recording of all people's deeds for that Great Day, what room is there for worry?

Then, it proceeds to state the most important program for human beings, saying: "O mankind, indeed the promise of Allah is truth" (إِنَّ وَعْدَ اللَّهِ حَقٌ‌).

The Resurrection, the reckoning, the scales, the punishment, the retribution, Paradise, and the Fire are all promises from the All-Powerful, All-Wise Allah that shall not be breached.

In light of this true promise, "let not the worldly life deceive you and let not the Deceiver [Satan] deceive you about Allah" (فَلا تَغُرَّنَّكُمُ الْحَياةُ الدُّنْيا وَ لا يَغُرَّنَّكُمْ بِاللَّهِ الْغَرُورُ).

Yes, the distracting factors and the alluring glitters of this world seek to fill your entire heart and make you heedless of that great Divine promise.

The satans among jinn and men are constantly engaged in whispering through various means of deception; they too want to occupy your entire mind and divert you from that great Promised Day ahead of you. If their deception and whisperings prove effective, your entire life will be ruined and your hopes for salvation will vanish; so be wary of them as well.

The repetition of the warning to the people—not to be deceived by the whisperings of the satans nor by the world—is actually an indication that there are two ways for sin to penetrate a human being:

1- The deceptive manifestations of the world: prestige, glory, wealth, status, and various desires.

2- Being deceived by Divine forgiveness and grace. It is here that Satan, on one hand, beautifies the glitter of this world in the eyes of man, presenting it as a ready, attractive, lovable, and valuable commodity.

On the other hand, whenever man wishes to control himself against the intense allure and attraction of the world by remembering the Resurrection and the Great Court of the Lord, Satan deceives him regarding Allah's forgiveness and the vastness of His mercy, and consequently invites him to sin and rebellion.

He is heedless of the fact that just as Allah is the "Most Merciful of the merciful" in the position of mercy, He is the "Severest of punishers" in the position of punishment and retribution. His mercy never encourages sin, just as His wrath cannot be a cause for despair.

"Gharur" (on the weight of jasur) is an intensive form meaning an extraordinarily deceptive being, and what is meant by it here could be any deceptive factor, just as it could specifically refer to Satan.

Of course, the second meaning is more consistent with the following verse, especially since "deception and delusion" (ghurur) are repeatedly attributed to Satan in the verses of the Quran.

Some commentators provide an analysis here which is summarized as follows:

Those who face factors of deception are of three groups: A group so weak and fragile that they are deceived by the slightest thing.

A group stronger than these, who are not deceived by the glitter of the world alone, but are deceived if a powerful whisperer incites them and makes the evil of their deeds seem light to them; thus, fleeting pleasures on one hand and whisperings on the other encourage them to perform ugly acts.

The third group, who are even stronger and more substantial than these, are neither self-deceived nor can anyone else deceive them.

The phrase "let not the worldly life deceive you" refers to the first group, and the phrase "and let not the Deceiver deceive you about Allah" refers to the second group. As for the third group, they are actually included under the title: "Indeed, over My [believing] servants there is for you no authority" [1].

The next verse is a warning to all believers regarding the issue of Satan's whisperings mentioned in the previous verse, saying: "Indeed, Satan is an enemy to you; so take him as an enemy" (إِنَّ الشَّيْطانَ لَكُمْ عَدُوٌّ فَاتَّخِذُوهُ عَدُوًّا).

[^1]: Tafsir Fakhr Razi, Vol. 26, p. 5.

His enmity began from the first day of Adam's creation. When he was expelled from the presence of the Lord due to his refusal to submit to Allah's command to prostrate to Adam, he swore that he would forever gird himself with enmity toward Adam and his children, and he even requested a respite and long life from Allah for this purpose! He stands by his word and seizes the smallest opportunity to exercise enmity and strike a blow against you. Does reason allow you not to accept him as an enemy and to be heedless of him for even a moment? Let alone following the "footsteps of Satan" (khutuwat al-shaitan), or accepting him as a compassionate companion and a sincere friend? "Will you then take him and his offspring as allies instead of Me while they are enemies to you?" (Al-Kahf: 50).

Furthermore, he is an enemy who attacks you from every side, as he himself says: "Then I will come to them from before them and from behind them and on their right and on their left" (Al-A'raf: 17).

And especially since he is in an ambush where "he sees man while man does not see him": "Indeed, he sees you, he and his tribe, from where you do not see them" (Al-A'raf: 27).

Of course, this does not prevent your ability to defend yourself against his whisperings.

There is a beautiful expression in the counsels of the Lord to Moses son of Imran, as the Commander of the Faithful, Ali (عليه السلام), narrates that Allah said to Moses: "I have four recommendations for you; strive to preserve them:

First: As long as you do not see your sins forgiven, do not occupy yourself with the faults of others!

Second: As long as you do not see My treasures exhausted, do not worry about your provision!

Third: As long as you do not see My kingdom vanishing, do not place hope in anyone other than Me!

Fourth: As long as you do not see Satan dead, do not feel secure from his cunning!" [1] In any case, Satan's enmity toward the children of Adam is a matter mentioned in numerous verses of the Quran, and he is even repeatedly referred to as a "clear enemy" (aduwwun mubin) [2]. One must always be on guard against such an enemy.

In the continuation of the verse, for further emphasis, it adds: "He only invites his party that they may be among the companions of the Blaze" (إِنَّما يَدْعُوا حِزْبَهُ لِيَكُونُوا مِنْ أَصْحابِ السَّعِيرِ).

"Hizb" (party) originally means a group or assembly that possesses organization and intensity of action, but it is usually applied to any group or population that follows a specific program and goal.

[^1]: Safinat al-Bihar, Vol. 1, p. 501, under the root 'r-b-'. [^2]: Verses 161 and 208 of Al-Baqarah, 142 of Al-An'am, 22 of Al-A'raf, 5 of Yusuf, 60 of Ya-Sin, and 62 of Al-Zukhruf.

What is meant by the "Party of Satan" are his followers and those who are on his path.

Of course, Satan cannot make everyone an official member of his party and invite them to Hell; the members of his party are those mentioned in other verses of the Quran, possessing the following signs:

Those who have placed the collar of servitude and his guardianship (wilayah) around their necks: "His authority is only over those who take him as an ally" (Al-Nahl: 100).

"Satan has overcome them and made them forget the remembrance of Allah. Those are the party of Satan. Unquestionably, the party of Satan—they are the losers" (Al-Mujadila: 19).

It is interesting that in three instances in the Quranic verses, the "Party of Allah" (Hizbullah) is mentioned, and in three instances the "Party of Satan" (Hizbush-Shaitan), so that people may see in which party they enroll and of which party they are members.

In any case, it is natural where Satan invites his party: to pollution and sin, to the impurities of desires, to polytheism, rebellion, and oppression, and finally to the fire of Hell [1]. Further explanation regarding the characteristics of the "Party of Allah" and the "Party of Satan" will be given, Allah willing, under verse 22 of Surah Al-Mujadila.

In the final verse under discussion, it describes the end of the "Party of Allah" and the painful fate of the "Party of Satan" as follows: "Those who disbelieve will have a severe punishment, and those who believe and do righteous deeds will have forgiveness and great reward" (الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لَهُمْ عَذابٌ شَدِيدٌ وَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَ عَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ لَهُمْ مَغْفِرَةٌ وَ أَجْرٌ كَبِيرٌ).

It is noteworthy that in the above verse, regarding the deserving of punishment, it contents itself with the issue of "disbelief" (kufr), but regarding the issue of forgiveness and great reward, it does not consider "faith" (iman) sufficient, but adds "righteous deeds" to it. This is because disbelief alone is the cause of eternal punishment, but faith without action will not be the cause of salvation; rather, faith and action are, from one perspective, inseparable and companions to one another [1]. At the end of the above verse, it first speaks of forgiveness and then of great reward, because "forgiveness" actually washes the believers first, then prepares them to receive the "great reward"—in technical terms, the first is "purification" (takhliyah) and the second is "adornment" (tahliyah).

[^1]: It is also worth noting that the "lam" in liyakunu can be the "lam of cause" or the "lam of consequence." [^1]: The nunation (tanwin) in "maghfiratun" and "adhabun" is for magnification and emphasis, meaning a great forgiveness and a painful punishment.

Ayahs

أَفَمَن زُيِّنَ لَهُۥ سُوٓءُ عَمَلِهِۦ فَرَءَاهُ حَسَنًا ۖ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يُضِلُّ مَن يَشَآءُ وَيَهْدِى مَن يَشَآءُ ۖ فَلَا تَذْهَبْ نَفْسُكَ عَلَيْهِمْ حَسَرَٰتٍ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَلِيمٌۢ بِمَا يَصْنَعُونَ (٨)
Is someone the evil of whose conduct is presented as decorous to him, so he regards it as good.... Indeed Allah leads astray whomever He wishes, and guides whomever He wishes. So do not fret yourself to death regretting for them. Indeed Allah knows best what they do. (8)
وَٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَرْسَلَ ٱلرِّيَـٰحَ فَتُثِيرُ سَحَابًا فَسُقْنَـٰهُ إِلَىٰ بَلَدٍ مَّيِّتٍ فَأَحْيَيْنَا بِهِ ٱلْأَرْضَ بَعْدَ مَوْتِهَا ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ ٱلنُّشُورُ (٩)
It is Allah who sends the winds and they raise a cloud; then We drive it toward a dead land and with it revive the earth after its death. Likewise will be the resurrection [of the dead]. (9)
مَن كَانَ يُرِيدُ ٱلْعِزَّةَ فَلِلَّهِ ٱلْعِزَّةُ جَمِيعًا ۚ إِلَيْهِ يَصْعَدُ ٱلْكَلِمُ ٱلطَّيِّبُ وَٱلْعَمَلُ ٱلصَّـٰلِحُ يَرْفَعُهُۥ ۚ وَٱلَّذِينَ يَمْكُرُونَ ٱلسَّيِّـَٔاتِ لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ شَدِيدٌ ۖ وَمَكْرُ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُوَ يَبُورُ (١٠)
Whoever seeks honour [should know that] honour entirely belongs to Allah. To Him ascends the good word, and He elevates righteous conduct; as for those who devise evil schemes, there is a severe punishment for them, and their plotting shall come to naught. (10)

Commentary

Pure Words and Righteous Deeds Ascend to Allah!

Since the preceding verses divided people into two groups—"the group of believers" and "the group of disbelievers," or "the party of Allah and the enemies of Satan" and "the party of his followers"—the first verse under discussion describes one of the most important characteristics of these two groups, which is in fact the source of all their other programs. It says: "Is he, then, to whom the evil of his deed has been made fair-seeming, so that he considers it good, [like one who perceives realities as they are, distinguishing between the beautiful and the ugly]?" (أَفَمَنْ زُيِّنَ لَهُ سُوءُ عَمَلِهِ فَرَآهُ حَسَناً).

In truth, this issue is the key to all the misfortunes of misguided and stubborn peoples: their foul deeds appear beautiful to them because they harmonize with their lusts and darkened hearts.

Naturally, such a person neither accepts any admonition, nor is he ready to hear criticism, nor is he ever willing to change his course. He neither analyzes his own actions nor fears their consequences.

Furthermore, when the talk turns to beauty and ugliness, they attribute beauty to themselves and ugliness to the true believers! It may well be that when the stubborn disbelievers heard the previous verses regarding the party of Satan and their painful fate, they applied them to the true believers and considered themselves the manifestation of the party of Allah! This is a truly great tragedy and calamity.

But who makes the evil deeds of the wicked appear attractive to them? Allah? Or their base desires? Or Satan?

Undoubtedly, the primary agents are base desires and Satan. However, since Allah has created this effect within those actions, it can be attributed to Him. This is because when humans commit a sin, they initially feel distressed by their action while their primordial nature (Fitrah) is pure, their conscience is awake, and their intellect is realistic. But the more they repeat it, the more their distress diminishes.

Gradually, they reach a stage of indifference, and if they continue to repeat it, the ugliness becomes beautiful in their eyes, to the point where they consider it among their honors and virtues, even as they are drowning in the quagmire of misery.

Interestingly, when the Quran poses this question—"Is he, then, to whom the evil of his deed has been made fair-seeming, so that he considers it good..."—it does not explicitly mention the opposite side. It is as if it wants to give the listener a wide scope to visualize various opposites in their mind and understand more deeply. It is as if it wants to say: Is such a person like those who are realistic?!... Is such a person like the pure-hearted who are constantly engaged in self-reckoning?... Is there any hope of salvation for such a person? [^1]

Then, the Quran proceeds to explain the reason for the difference between these two groups, adding: "Indeed, Allah leaves to stray whom He wills and guides whom He wills." (فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ يُضِلُّ مَنْ يَشاءُ وَ يَهْدِي مَنْ يَشاءُ).

If the evil deeds of the first group have become beautified in their eyes, this is the result of Divine "misguidance" (Idlal). It is He who placed this property in the repetition of evil deeds, such that the human soul becomes accustomed to them, habituated, and eventually harmonized with them. And it is He who grants the pure-hearted believers piercing and insightful eyes and hearing ears to perceive realities as they truly are.

It is clear that this Divine Will is coupled with His Wisdom, and He grants to everyone what they deserve. Therefore, at the end of the verse, He says: "So let not your soul go out [in grief] for them in sighs of regret." (فَلا تَذْهَبْ نَفْسُكَ عَلَيْهِمْ حَسَراتٍ).

This expression is similar to what appears in Surah Ash-Shu'ara, verse 3: "Perhaps you would kill yourself [with grief] that they will not become believers." (لَعَلَّكَ باخِعٌ نَفْسُكَ أَلَّا يَكُونُوا مُؤْمِنِينَ) [^2]. The use of the term "Hasarat" (regrets/sighs), which is grammatically a "Maf'ul la-ajlih" (object of purpose) for the preceding sentence, indicates that you do not just feel one regret for them, but rather many regrets.

Regret for their loss of the blessing of guidance; regret for wasting the jewel of humanity; regret for losing the sense of discernment to the point that they see the ugly as beautiful; and finally, regret for being caught in the fire of the Lord's wrath and anger! But why should you not regret? "Because Allah is All-Knowing of what they do [and He will give them exactly what they deserve]." (إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَلِيمٌ بِما يَصْنَعُونَ).

From the tone of the verse, the extraordinary compassion of the Prophet of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله toward the misguided and deviant is completely evident. Such is the state of a true Divine leader, who suffers so much from people's rejection of the truth, their submission to falsehood, and their turning their backs on all means of prosperity, that it is as if he is about to expire from grief.

In the following verse, following the previous discussions on guidance, misguidance, faith, and disbelief, it provides a brief and clear explanation regarding the Origin (Mabda') and the Return (Ma'ad). It pairs the proof of the "Origin" with the proof of the "Return" in an interesting argument, saying: "And it is Allah who sends the winds so that they stir up clouds," (وَ اللَّهُ الَّذِي أَرْسَلَ الرِّياحَ فَتُثِيرُ سَحاباً) [^1] "then We drive them to a dead and dry land," (فَسُقْناهُ إِلى‌ بَلَدٍ مَيِّتٍ).

[^1]: From here it becomes clear that the verse has an implied clause, which might be: "Is he like one who is not so... like one who reckons with his soul and sees the ugly as ugly... can any reform or repentance be hoped for him?"

[^2]: Another commentary has been mentioned for the above verse: that the Prophet should not be distressed by the intensity of their harassment and opposition, for Allah knows their deeds well and will take vengeance upon them in due time.

[^1]: Regarding why the first verb is in the past tense (arsala) and the second in the present/future (fathuthiru), and why one is in the third person (arsala) and the other in the first person (fasuqnahu), commentators have mentioned several reasons. However, since they did not seem precise, they were omitted; it may simply be for stylistic variation and diversity in speech.

"And thereby We give life to the earth after its death." (فأَحْيَيْنا بِهِ الْأَرْضَ بَعْدَ مَوْتِها). "Even so is the Resurrection [of the dead after death]!" (كذلك النشور).

The calculated system governing the movement of the winds, then the movement of the clouds, followed by the descent of life-giving drops of rain, and subsequently the revival of dead lands, is itself the best proof and witness to the fact that the hand of a Wise Power lies behind this mechanism and manages it.

First, He commands the warm and hot winds to move from equatorial regions toward cold regions, evaporating the seawater in their path and sending it into the sky. Then, He commands the regular cold polar currents, which constantly move in the opposite direction of the first current, to collect the resulting vapors and form clouds.

Again, He commands those same winds to carry the clouds on their backs and dispatch them toward dead deserts, so that life-giving drops of rain may pour down from them. Then, He commands the specific conditions of the earth and the seeds of plants scattered within it to accept the water, grow, and sprout. From an apparently lowly and worthless substance, He brings forth living beings that are highly diverse, beautiful, lush, green, useful, and fruitful—which is both a proof of His Power, a witness to His Wisdom, and a sign of the Great Resurrection!

In fact, the above verse invites to Monotheism (Tawhid) from several perspectives: From the perspective of the Argument from Order; from the perspective of the Argument from Motion (that every moving object requires a mover); and from the perspective of mentioning blessings, which serves as an innate motivation to thank the Benefactor.

It is also a proof for the issue of the Resurrection from several aspects: From the perspective of the evolutionary path of beings, and from the perspective of the appearance of life from dead earth. That is to say: O human, the scene of the Resurrection is before your eyes and under your feet in every season of the year.

It is also necessary to note that the phrase "fathuthiru" is from the root "itharah," meaning to spread and scatter. Here it refers to the production of clouds due to the blowing of winds over the surface of the oceans, as the issue of the movement of clouds is mentioned in the subsequent phrase (فَسُقْناهُ إِلى‌ بَلَدٍ مَيِّتٍ).

Interestingly, we read in a Hadith from the Prophet of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله that one of his companions asked: "O Messenger of Allah! How does the Lord bring the dead to life, and what is the sign of that in His creation?" The Prophet replied: "Have you not passed by the valley of your tribe while it was barren and dead, and then passed by it again while it was vibrating with greenness?" I said: "Yes, O Messenger of Allah." He said: "Even so does Allah bring the dead to life, and that is His sign in creation." [^1]

In Volume 16 of Tafsir Nemouneh, under verse 48 of Surah Ar-Rum, we had another discussion on this subject.

[^1]: Tafsir al-Qurtubi, Vol. 8, p. 5409 (under the verses discussed).

Following this monotheistic discussion, the verse points to the great mistake of the polytheists who sought their honor from idols and saw accepting faith in the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله as a cause for people to scatter from around them, saying: "If we follow the guidance with you, we would be snatched away from our land" (Qasas: 57).

It says: "Whoever desires honor, then to Allah belongs all honor." (مَنْ كانَ يُرِيدُ الْعِزَّةَ فَلِلَّهِ الْعِزَّةُ جَمِيعاً).

"Izzah" (honor/might), according to Raghib in Al-Mufradat, is originally that state which makes a person resistant and invincible. Hard and firm ground is also called Azaz for this reason.

Since only His Pure Essence is invincible—otherwise all creatures are susceptible to defeat by virtue of their limitations—therefore, all honor belongs to Him. Anyone who acquires honor does so from the blessing of His infinite ocean.

In a Hadith narrated from Anas, the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: "Your Lord says every day: 'I am the Mighty (Al-Aziz), so whoever desires the honor of the two worlds, let him obey the Mighty!'"

In truth, an aware person must take water from the source, where the water is pure and abundant, not from small and meager vessels that are both limited and contaminated, and are in the hands of this person or that.

In the accounts of Imam Hasan ibn Ali عليه السلام, we read that in the final hours of his life, when one of his companions named Junadah ibn Abi Sufyan asked him for advice, he gave him valuable and effective counsel, including: "If you desire honor without a tribe and awe without a kingdom, then exit from the humiliation of disobeying Allah into the honor of obeying Allah!" [^1]

And if we see that in some verses of the Quran, "honor" is attributed to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله and the believers in addition to Allah—"And to Allah belongs honor, and to His Messenger, and to the believers" (Munafiqun: 8)—it is because they too have acquired honor from the reflection of the Lord's honor and walk in the path of His obedience.

Then, it describes the way to attain "honor" as follows: "To Him ascends the pure word," (إِلَيْهِ يَصْعَدُ الْكَلِمُ الطَّيِّبُ). "And the righteous deed, He lifts it up." (وَ الْعَمَلُ الصَّالِحُ يَرْفَعُهُ).

"Al-Kalim al-Tayyib" means pure words. The purity of a word lies in the purity of its content, and the purity of content is due to concepts that correspond to pure and radiant objective realities. And what reality is higher than the Pure Essence of Allah, His religion of truth and justice, and the good and pure ones who walk in the path of spreading it? Therefore, "Al-Kalim al-Tayyib" has been interpreted as correct beliefs regarding the Origin, the Return, and the religion of God.

Yes, it is such a pure belief that soars toward Allah and also makes its possessor fly, until they reside in the proximity of the Truth and are immersed in the honor of the Mighty Lord.

Undoubtedly, from this pure root grow branches whose fruit is righteous deeds. Every worthy, useful, and constructive act—whether it be inviting to the truth, supporting the oppressed, fighting the oppressor and tyrant, self-purification and worship, education and training—in short, everything included in this vast and expansive concept, if performed for Allah and for His pleasure, will also soar and ascend to the heaven of the Lord's grace. It becomes the means of the spiritual ascension (Mi'raj) and perfection of its doer, and their enjoyment of the Truth's honor.

This is the same thing referred to in Surah Ibrahim, verse 24: "Have you not seen how Allah sets forth a parable? A pure word is like a pure tree, whose root is firmly fixed and whose branches reach the sky, yielding its fruit at all times by the permission of its Lord."

From what we have said, it becomes clear that although some commentators have interpreted "pure word" as La ilaha illa Allah, and others as Subhan Allah, wal-Hamdu lillah, wa La ilaha illa Allah, wa Allahu Akbar, and some as including Muhammadun Rasulullah and Aliyun Waliyyullah, or in some narrations "pure word" and "righteous deed" are interpreted as the Wilayah of the Ahl al-Bayt عليهم السلام or the like—all of these are by way of explaining the clearest manifestations of that vast and expansive concept. They do not create a limitation in its meaning, for every word that has pure and sublime content is gathered under this title.

In any case, the same God who, according to the previous verse, revives the dead earth with life-giving drops of rain, also nurtures the "pure word" and "righteous deed" and brings them to the proximity of His closeness and mercy.

Then, turning to the opposite point, it says: "And those who plot evil deeds, for them is a severe punishment," (وَ الَّذِينَ يَمْكُرُونَ السَّيِّئاتِ لَهُمْ عَذابٌ شَدِيدٌ). "And the plotting of those—it will perish [and lead to nothing]." (وَ مَكْرُ أُولئِكَ هُوَ يَبُورُ).

Although these corrupt evildoers imagine that through oppression, lies, and deception they can acquire honor, wealth, and power for themselves, in the end, they have both prepared a severe Divine punishment for themselves and their efforts will vanish into thin air.

There were those who, as the Quran says, "took man-made gods as a source of honor for themselves" (Maryam: 81). And there were hypocrites who thought themselves honorable and the believers humiliated, saying: "If we return to Medina, the more honorable will surely expel therefrom the more humiliated!" (Munafiqun: 8).

There were others who imagined proximity to the Pharaohs as the source of their honor, or sought prestige through sin and oppression, but they all fell. Only faith and righteous deeds ascend toward the Mighty Allah! "Makr" (plotting), although linguistically meaning any kind of planning or resourcefulness, is used in some instances to mean planning coupled with corruption, which is the case in the verse under discussion.

"Sayyi'at" (evil deeds) in the above verse includes all ugliness and evils, whether in belief or action. The fact that some have interpreted it as the conspiracies of the polytheists to kill or exile the Prophet of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله from Mecca is actually stating one of its manifestations, not its entire meaning.

The word "yabur" is from the root "bawar" and "buran," which originally means extreme stagnation. Since such stagnation leads to destruction, this word is used to mean ruin and perishing. There is a well-known proverb: "It stagnated until it became rotten!"

Key Points

1- All "Honor" Belongs to Allah! What is the reality of honor (Izzah)? Is it anything other than reaching a stage of invincibility? If so, where should honor be sought, and what can give a person honor?

In a clear analysis, we conclude that the reality of honor is primarily a power that appears in the heart and soul of a person, preventing them from submission and compromise before tyrants and rebels. It is a power with which one is never enslaved by lusts and does not bow before base desires. It is a power that elevates one to a stage of being impenetrable by "gold" (wealth) and "force."

Does this power stem from anything other than faith in Allah—that is, connection to the primary source of power and honor?

This is at the level of thought, belief, and spirit. As for the level of action, honor stems from deeds that have a correct root and a calculated program and method; in other words, it can be summarized as "righteous deeds." These two give a person high-standing and greatness, and grant them honor and invincibility.

The "world-worshipping sorcerers" contemporary to Pharaoh began their tricks in his name and by his honor: "They said: 'By the honor of Pharaoh, indeed it is we who will be the victors!'" (Shu'ara: 44). But they were soon defeated by the shepherd's staff of Moses عليه السلام.

However, when those same people came out from under the humiliating banner of Pharaoh, took refuge in the shadow of Monotheism, and believed, they became so powerful and invincible that Pharaoh's harshest threats had no effect on them. They lovingly gave their hands, feet, and even their lives in the way of Allah and drank the nectar of martyrdom. By this act, they showed that they would not submit to wealth or force and were invincible. Their glorious history today is a world of instructive lessons for us.

2- The Difference Between "Pure Word" and "Righteous Deed" A question may arise: why does the verse say regarding the "pure word" that it ascends to the Lord by itself, but regarding the "righteous deed," it says Allah lifts it up?

This question can be answered as follows: The "pure word," as we said, refers to faith and pure belief, and that is the very essence of soaring toward Allah—for the reality of faith is nothing but this. But the "righteous deed" is what He accepts, rewards manifold, grants permanence and duration, and elevates (reflect on this).

Ayahs

وَٱللَّهُ خَلَقَكُم مِّن تُرَابٍ ثُمَّ مِن نُّطْفَةٍ ثُمَّ جَعَلَكُمْ أَزْوَٰجًا ۚ وَمَا تَحْمِلُ مِنْ أُنثَىٰ وَلَا تَضَعُ إِلَّا بِعِلْمِهِۦ ۚ وَمَا يُعَمَّرُ مِن مُّعَمَّرٍ وَلَا يُنقَصُ مِنْ عُمُرِهِۦٓ إِلَّا فِى كِتَـٰبٍ ۚ إِنَّ ذَٰلِكَ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ يَسِيرٌ (١١)
Allah created you from dust, then from a drop of [seminal] fluid, then He made you mates. No female conceives or delivers except with His knowledge, and no elderly person advances in years, nor is anything diminished of his life, but it is [recorded] in a Book. That is indeed easy for Allah. (11)
وَمَا يَسْتَوِى ٱلْبَحْرَانِ هَـٰذَا عَذْبٌ فُرَاتٌ سَآئِغٌ شَرَابُهُۥ وَهَـٰذَا مِلْحٌ أُجَاجٌ ۖ وَمِن كُلٍّ تَأْكُلُونَ لَحْمًا طَرِيًّا وَتَسْتَخْرِجُونَ حِلْيَةً تَلْبَسُونَهَا ۖ وَتَرَى ٱلْفُلْكَ فِيهِ مَوَاخِرَ لِتَبْتَغُوا۟ مِن فَضْلِهِۦ وَلَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ (١٢)
Not alike are the two seas: this one sweet and agreeable, pleasant to drink, and that one briny and bitter, and from each you eat fresh meat and obtain ornaments, which you wear. And you see the ships plowing through them, that you may seek of His bounty, and so that you may give thanks. (12)

Commentary

The Seas of Fresh and Salt Water Are Not Alike!

Considering that the previous verses discussed the issues of Monotheism, the Resurrection, and the Attributes of Allah, the verses under discussion explain another part of the "Signs within the soul" (Anfusi) and "Signs in the horizons" (Afaqi). These serve as evidence of Allah's power on one hand, His knowledge on the other, and the possibility of the Resurrection on a third.

First, it refers to the creation of man in various stages, stating: "And Allah created you from dust" (وَ اللَّهُ خَلَقَكُمْ مِنْ تُرابٍ‌).

"Then from a sperm-drop" (ثُمَّ مِنْ نُطْفَةٍ).

"Then He made you pairs" (ثُمَّ جَعَلَكُمْ أَزْواجاً).

These are three stages of human creation: dust, the sperm-drop, and the stage of pairing (marriage).

It is certain that man is from dust, both in the sense that the ancestor of humanity, Adam, was created from dust, and in the sense that all the materials that constitute the human body, or that man consumes for nourishment, or from which his sperm is formed, ultimately trace back to the elements hidden within the earth.

Some have suggested that creation from dust refers only to the initial creation, while creation from the sperm-drop refers to subsequent stages—the first being the general stage of human creation (since the existence of all was summarized in the existence of Adam) and the second being the detailed stage that distinguishes them from one another.

In any case, the stage of "pairing" is the stage of the continuity of the human race and its multiplication. The suggestion by some that "pairs" (azwaj) here means "species" or "soul and body" and the like seems very far-fetched.

Then, entering the fourth and fifth stages of human life, the subject of "mothers' pregnancies" and their "delivery" is brought forward, stating: "And no female conceives nor does she deliver except with His knowledge" (وَ ما تَحْمِلُ مِنْ أُنْثى‌ وَ لا تَضَعُ إِلَّا بِعِلْمِهِ‌).

Indeed, the matter of "pregnancy" and the incredibly strange and complex transformations of the fetus, followed by reaching the stage of delivery and the wonderful changes that occur at that sensitive and critical moment to the mother on one hand and the fetus on the other, is so delicate and precise that it is impossible without reliance on the infinite knowledge of Allah. If the governing system over it were disrupted by even a hair's breadth, the program of pregnancy or delivery would fall into chaos or dysfunction, leading to ruin and destruction.

Each of these five stages of human life is more wondrous and amazing than the other.

Where is lifeless, dead dust, and where is a living, rational, conscious, and innovative human?! Where is the insignificant sperm-drop, consisting of a few drops of base fluid, and where is a mature, beautiful human equipped with various senses and diverse systems?! ^1 Moving past this stage, the issue of dividing the human species into two genders, "male" and "female," with vast differences in body and soul and physiological matters, comes into play. From the very beginning of the sperm's fertilization, they separate their paths, and each

^1: "Nutfah" (sperm-drop), as we have previously mentioned, originally means pure water or a small amount of pure water; subsequently, it was applied to the small amount of fluid that becomes the starting point for the formation of the fetus.

proceeds toward the mission assigned to them and reaches perfection.

Then comes the matter of the mother's mission in accepting and bearing this burden, and its protection, nourishment, and upbringing, which has attracted the thoughts of great scientists for centuries, and they confess that it is among the most wondrous matters of the universe.

The final stage in this section, which is the stage of birth, is a revolutionary and completely critical stage accompanied by many wonders:

What factors command the fetus to exit the mother's womb?

How is perfect coordination established between this command and the preparation of the mother's organs to execute it?

How can the fetus completely change the condition it has been accustomed to for nine months in just one moment, sever its connection with the mother, utilize open air, have its food passage through the umbilical cord suddenly closed, and have the new passage—the mouth—begin to function, leave the dark environment of the mother's womb to be immersed in light and brightness, and resist all these transformations and adapt itself immediately?! Are these not the best signs of Allah's infinite power and knowledge? And does mindless matter and aimless nature, using "blind coincidences," have the possibility of organizing even one small link among the thousands of links in the chain of creation? How unfair it is for a human to accept such a baseless assumption regarding his own creation!

Then, the "sixth" and "seventh" stages of this magnificent program are addressed in another link, referring to the various stages of life and its increase or decrease due to various factors, stating:

"And no aged person is granted [long] life, nor is anything diminished from his life, but it is in a Record [of Allah's knowledge]" and follows laws and programs governed by His knowledge and power (وَ ما يُعَمَّرُ مِنْ مُعَمَّرٍ وَ لا يُنْقَصُ مِنْ عُمُرِهِ إِلَّا فِي كِتابٍ‌) ^1. What factors are effective in prolonging human life? What factors fight against the continuation of his life? In short, what factors must join hands so that a human can continue his life for a hundred years, or more or less? And finally, what factors cause the difference in the lifespans of humans?

All of these also involve precise and complex calculations of which none but Allah is aware, and what we know today in these fields is very little and insignificant compared to what we do not know.

"Mu'ammar" (the aged) is derived from the root "Umr," which originally comes from "Imarah," meaning prosperity or habitation. The duration of human life is called "Umr" because the prosperity and habitation of his body occur during this period.

The word "Mu'ammar" refers to someone who has a long life.

Finally, the verse ends with this sentence: "Indeed, that is easy for Allah" (إِنَّ ذلِكَ عَلَى اللَّهِ يَسِيرٌ).

The creation of this wondrous being from "dust," and the beginning of a complete human's creation from "sperm-water," as well as matters related to gender, pairing, pregnancy, delivery, and the increase and decrease of life—whether in terms of power or in terms of knowledge and calculation—are all easy and simple for Him. These are a portion of the "Signs within the soul" (Anfusi) that, on one hand, connect and familiarize us with the Origin of the universe and, on the other hand, are considered living proofs for the possibility of the

^1: What is meant by "Kitab" (Record/Book) is the infinite knowledge of Allah, and the fact that some have interpreted it as the "Preserved Tablet" (Lawh al-Mahfuz) or the "Record of Human Life" also traces back to Allah's knowledge.

Resurrection.

Is He who was capable of the initial creation from "dust" and "sperm" not capable of restoring the life of humans?

And does He who is aware of all the intricacies related to these laws have any difficulty in maintaining the account of the servants' deeds for the scene of the Resurrection?!

In the next verse, another part of the "Signs in the horizons" (Afaqi), which are signs of His greatness and power, is mentioned regarding the creation of the seas and their blessings and benefits: "And the two seas are not alike: this one is palatable and sweet, pleasant to drink, and that one is salty and bitter" (وَ ما يَستَوِي البَحرانِ هذا عَذبٌ فُراتٌ سائِغٌ شَرابُهُ وَ هذا مِلحٌ أُجاجٌ‌). ^1 Even though both initially descended from the sky to the earth as drops of sweet and palatable rain, and both are derived from one root, they have appeared in two completely different forms with different benefits.

And amazingly, "from each you eat fresh meat" (وَ مِنْ كُلٍّ تَأْكُلُونَ لَحْماً طَرِيًّا).

"And from each you extract ornaments which you wear" (وَ تَسْتَخْرِجُونَ حِلْيَةً تَلْبَسُونَها).

Furthermore, from both you can benefit for your transport and goods; therefore, "and you see the ships therein cleaving through [the waves] from every side, that you may seek of His bounty, and perhaps you will be grateful" (وَ تَرَى الْفُلْكَ فِيهِ مَواخِرَ لِتَبْتَغُوا مِنْ فَضْلِهِ وَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ‌).

Pay attention to these few points

1- "Furat," according to Lisan al-Arab, is water that is at the peak of "Adhubat" (purity and sweetness).

"Sa'igh" refers to water that, due to its pleasantness, goes down the throat easily, unlike "Milh" (salty water) and "Ujaj," which is bitter water that seemingly burns the throat and blocks the passage of the gullet!

2- A group of commentators believe that the verse is an example of the inequality between the "believer" and the "disbeliever." However, the preceding and succeeding verses, which all speak of the signs of creation, and even the conclusion of this very verse, testify to the fact that this sentence also discusses the secrets of Monotheism and refers to the diversity of waters, their different effects, and their shared benefits.

3- In this verse, three benefits out of the many benefits of the seas are stated: food materials, ornaments, and the issue of transportation.

We know that the sea is an important source of human food, and every year millions of tons of fresh meat are taken from it without man having to endure labor or hardship for it. The system of creation has made precise planning in this regard so that humans can benefit from this boundless table of blessings and the spread Divine banquet with the least effort.

Various ornaments such as "pearls," "shells," "gems," and "coral" are extracted from it. The Quran's emphasis on this matter is because the human soul, unlike animals, possesses various dimensions, one of which is the sense of beauty, which is the source of the emergence of aesthetic, artistic, and literary matters. Satisfying this in a correct manner,

away from any excess, negligence, extravagance, or waste, refreshes the soul, gives man vitality and peace, and prepares him for performing the heavy tasks of life.

As for the issue of transportation, which is one of the most important foundations of human civilization and social life, considering that the seas cover a major part of the earth and are interconnected, they can provide the most important service to humans in this field.

The volume of goods transported by sea, and the passengers moved by it, is so great that it is not comparable to any other means of transport, just as sometimes one ship can carry as much cargo as tens of thousands of automobiles! ^1

4- Of course, the benefits of the seas are not limited to the above matters, and the Quran does not intend to restrict them to these three affairs. The formation of clouds, medicinal materials, oil, clothing materials, strengthening materials for barren lands, influence on the creation of winds, and others are considered other blessings of the seas.

5- The Quran's emphasis on "lahman tariyya" (fresh meat) is a meaningful reference to the benefits of consuming such meats compared to the harms of old meats, canned goods, and the like.

6- Here a question arises: Saltwater seas are spread across the entire globe, but where is the freshwater sea?

^1: Currently, 500,000-ton ships have been built for the transport of oil products, which no other means of transport can replace, and no road except the seas has the capacity to bear them. In the past as well, the power of ships was much greater than that of pack animals.

In response, it must be said: Freshwater seas and lakes are also not few on the globe, such as the freshwater lakes of the United States and others. Furthermore, great rivers are sometimes called "Bahr" (sea), just as in the story of Moses, the word "Bahr" was applied to the great Nile River (Al-Baqarah: 50, Ash-Shu'ara: 63, Al-A'raf: 138).

Beyond this, the advancement of the water of great rivers into the seas—considering that they push back the salt waters and do not mix with them for a while—themselves form vast seas of fresh water.

7- The phrase "that you may seek of His bounty" (لِتَبْتَغُوا مِنْ فَضْلِهِ) has a broad meaning that includes any economic activity carried out through maritime routes.

And the phrase "perhaps you will be grateful" (لَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ) is intended to awaken the sense of gratitude in humans, which is a means for seeking and knowing God.

Key Point: 1- Spiritual Factors of Longevity and Its Shortness

In line with the discussion in the above verses regarding the increase and decrease of life by the command of the Lord, a group of commentators, following the narrations transmitted in this regard, have discussed the length of life and its shortness.

Certainly, a series of natural factors play a role in increasing or shortening life, many of which have been recognized by humans so far, such as proper nutrition away from excess or deficiency, continuous work and movement, avoidance of any narcotics, dangerous addictions, and alcoholic beverages, staying away from constant excitement, and having strong faith that can grant man peace and strength in the hardships of life.

However, in addition to these, there are factors whose apparent connection with the issue of longevity is not very clear to us, but they are precisely emphasized in Islamic narrations. As an example, consider the following few narrations:

A- The Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله says: "Indeed, charity and maintaining family ties (silat al-rahim) prosper the lands and increase lifespans!" ^1

B- In another hadith from the same noble figure, it is stated: "Whoever is pleased that his sustenance be expanded and his death be delayed, let him maintain his family ties." [^2]

C- Regarding some sins like adultery (zina), it is specifically mentioned that it reduces human life, including in the famous hadith of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله who says: "O assembly of Muslims, beware of adultery, for in it are six consequences: three in this world and three in the Hereafter. As for those in this world: it takes away the radiance [of the face], brings about poverty, and diminishes life." [^3]

D- Imam al-Baqir عليه السلام says: "Righteousness and secret charity dispel poverty, increase life, and prevent seventy types of bad deaths." [^4]

Regarding some other sins like oppression, and indeed sins in general, there are indications in the hadiths.

Some commentators who have been unable to distinguish between the "fixed term" (ajal hatmi) and the "suspended term" (ajal mu'allaq) have attacked such hadiths and considered them contrary to Quranic texts that consider the limit of human life fixed and unchangeable ^1.

To explain: Undoubtedly, man has two types of terms and deadlines: The fixed term (ajal hatmi), which is the end of the human body's potential for survival, and upon its arrival, everything ends by Divine command. The suspended term (ajal mu'allaq), which changes with the change of conditions. For example, a person commits suicide, whereas if he had not committed this major sin, he would have lived for years. Or, due to turning to alcoholic beverages, narcotics, and limitless lust, he loses his physical strength in a short time, whereas if these things were not present, he could have lived for many years.

These are matters that are perceivable and experiential for everyone, and no one can deny them. In the field of unintended accidents as well, there are matters related to the suspended term that are also undeniable. Therefore, if it is stated in numerous narrations that charity in the way of Allah or maintaining family ties lengthens life and removes calamities, it is in fact referring to these factors.

And if we do not separate these two types of terms and lifespans, the understanding of many issues regarding "Divine Decree and Destiny" (Qada and Qadar) and "the impact of struggle and effort

^1, [^2], [^3]: Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 4, pp. 354 and 355. [^4]: Safinat al-Bihar, Vol. 2, p. 23, under the root "Sadaqah." ^1: Tafsir al-Alusi, Vol. 22, p. 164 (under the verses discussed).

in human lives" will remain unresolved.

This discussion can be clarified through a simple example: A person acquires a new automobile which, according to various predictions used in its construction, can live, for example, twenty years, but on the condition that it is precisely maintained and necessary care is taken. In this case, the fixed term of this car is those twenty years, which it will not exceed.

But if the necessary care is not taken, and it is handed over to ignorant and reckless individuals who work it beyond its power and capacity, and use it every day on rocky roads, its twenty-year life might decrease to half or a tenth. This is its "suspended term."

And we wonder how some famous commentators have not paid attention to a clear matter such as this.

Ayahs

يُولِجُ ٱلَّيْلَ فِى ٱلنَّهَارِ وَيُولِجُ ٱلنَّهَارَ فِى ٱلَّيْلِ وَسَخَّرَ ٱلشَّمْسَ وَٱلْقَمَرَ كُلٌّ يَجْرِى لِأَجَلٍ مُّسَمًّى ۚ ذَٰلِكُمُ ٱللَّهُ رَبُّكُمْ لَهُ ٱلْمُلْكُ ۚ وَٱلَّذِينَ تَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِهِۦ مَا يَمْلِكُونَ مِن قِطْمِيرٍ (١٣)
He makes the night pass into the day and makes the day pass into the night, and He has disposed the sun and the moon, each moving for a specified term. That is Allah, your Lord; to Him belongs all sovereignty. As for those whom you invoke besides Him, they do not control so much as the husk of a date stone. (13)
إِن تَدْعُوهُمْ لَا يَسْمَعُوا۟ دُعَآءَكُمْ وَلَوْ سَمِعُوا۟ مَا ٱسْتَجَابُوا۟ لَكُمْ ۖ وَيَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ يَكْفُرُونَ بِشِرْكِكُمْ ۚ وَلَا يُنَبِّئُكَ مِثْلُ خَبِيرٍ (١٤)
If you invoke them they will not hear your invocation, and even if they heard they cannot respond to you, and on the Day of Resurrection they will forswear your polytheism, and none can inform you like the One who is all-aware. (14)

Commentary

These False Deities Do Not Even Hear Your Voice!

Once again, these verses point to another part of the signs of Monotheism (Tawhid) and the endless blessings of the Lord, in order to awaken the sense of gratitude within humans while providing awareness, guiding them toward recognizing the True Deity, and restraining them from any form of polytheism and superstitious worship. He says: "He causes the night to enter the day, and He causes the day to enter the night" (يُولِجُ اللَّيْلَ فِي النَّهارِ وَ يُولِجُ النَّهارِ فِي اللَّيْلِ).

"Yuliju" is derived from the root "Iylaj," meaning "to enter" or "to insert." It may refer to one of the two following meanings, or both: the gradual increase and decrease of night and day throughout the year, which is the source of the emergence of the different seasons with all their effects and blessings; or the gradual transition from night to day and from day to night through the existence of twilight and the period between dawn and sunrise, which prevents the dangers of a sudden transition from darkness to light and from light to darkness, providing humans with sufficient preparation for a completely calm and safe transition from one to the other. [^1] Then, referring to the subjugation of the sun and the moon, He says: "and He has subjected the sun and the moon" (وَ سَخَّرَ الشَّمْسَ وَ الْقَمَرَ).

What subjugation could be greater or higher than this—that they all move in the path of human interests and are the source of various blessings in human life? The clouds, the wind, the moon, the sun, and the celestial spheres are all at work so that man may provide for his life and not fall into heedlessness, but rather remember the Original Source of these bounties.
